disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Inflammation strictly involves the cartilaginous ear and SPARES the (non-cartilaginous) earlobe
trigger_question_examples: Is the soft fleshy earlobe at the bottom involved, or only the firm upper cartilage part?
Where exactly is the redness/swelling — the whole ear or just the cartilage?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Only the firm upper cartilage part is red and swollen; the fleshy earlobe is completely normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no ear-canal discharge, no pain/swelling behind the ear over the mastoid, no ear protrusion, no recent piercing/trauma (against mastoiditis/bacterial perichondritis)
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, discharge from the ear canal, or pain/swelling behind the ear over the bone with the ear pushed forward?
Any recent ear piercing or injury?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, no discharge, nothing swollen behind the ear or pushing it forward, and no recent piercing or injury.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No hoarseness, throat/airway pain, breathing difficulty, or red eyes (other-cartilage/systemic a cartilage-inflammation condition involvement)
trigger_question_examples: Any hoarseness, pain over your windpipe, breathing difficulty, or red painful eyes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No hoarseness, no windpipe pain, my breathing is fine, and no red eyes or nose pain.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a spreading brain infection? Do I need antibiotics or hospital?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that earlobe sparing points to cartilage inflammation (a cartilage-inflammation condition) rather than a bacterial infection/mastoiditis, but because cartilage infection (perichondritis) threatens the cartilage and cannot be fully excluded remotely, arrange urgent same/next-day in-person assessment alongside NSAIDs/cold compress, with a firm mastoiditis/airway safety-net.
